q.en: When and where was George Washington born?
q.zh: 乔治·华盛顿何时、何地出生？

options.en: 1759 in Mount Vernon according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 1759年，弗农山庄（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: 'George Washington was born on February 22, 1732... on a large farm in Virginia.'
explain.zh: 书里说：“乔治·华盛顿于1732年2月22日出生……在弗吉尼亚一个大农场。”

q.en: At what event did Washington cross the Delaware River on a cold Christmas night in 1776?
q.zh: 1776年寒冷圣诞夜，华盛顿在哪次行动中渡过了特拉华河？

options.en: His inauguration according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他的就职典礼（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A surprise attack on the British in New Jersey

explain.en: 'On a cold Christmas night in 1776, Washington led his men across the river in a surprise attack. It led to an important victory.'
explain.zh: 书里说：“1776年一个寒冷的圣诞夜，华盛顿率部下渡河发起突袭。这次行动带来了重要胜利。”

q.en: In the glossary, the word 'treaty' means—

options.en: a speech according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一场演讲（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: a formal agreement of peace or friendship between two nations or groups
options.zh: 两国或两群体之间和平或友谊的正式协定

explain.en: Glossary: 'treaty (n.) a formal agreement of peace or friendship between two nations or groups (p. 12).'
explain.zh: 词汇表：treaty 意为“两国或两群体之间和平或友谊的正式协定（第12页）”。

q.en: What did Washington do about slavery?
q.zh: 华盛顿对奴隶制采取了什么态度？

options.en: He owned no slaves according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他没有奴隶（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He felt slavery was wrong and ordered his slaves freed upon Martha's death
options.zh: 他认为奴隶制是错误的，并下令在玛莎去世后解放他的奴隶

explain.en: 'Although the Washingtons owned more than three hundred slaves, George felt that slavery was wrong. He ordered that his slaves should be set free upon Martha's death.'
explain.zh: 书里说：“尽管华盛顿家拥有三百多名奴隶，乔治却认为奴隶制错误。他下令在玛莎去世后解放他的奴隶。”

q.en: In what year did Washington become the first president?
q.zh: 华盛顿在哪一年成为首任总统？

options.en: 1776 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: He 'became president of the United States on April 30' 1789, after leaving Mount Vernon that spring.
explain.zh: 书里说他在那年春天离开弗农山庄后，“于4月30日成为美国总统”，即1789年。

options.en: Whiskey makers angry about being taxed, which Washington put down with the military
options.zh: 酿酒者因被征税而愤怒，华盛顿派军队平息

options.en: A war with France according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 与法国的战争（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: 'He faced a major problem in his second term with the Whiskey Rebellion. People who made whiskey were angry that the government was taxing them. Washington had to send in the military to calm them.'
explain.zh: 书里说：“第二任期他遇到威士忌叛乱的大麻烦。酿酒者因政府征税而愤怒。华盛顿不得不派军队平息。”

q.en: What advice did Washington give in his Farewell Address?
q.zh: 华盛顿在《告别演说》中给出了什么建议？

options.en: To always go to war according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 永远开战（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To avoid joining forces with other nations and building too big an army
options.zh: 避免与其他国家结盟，也避免建立过于庞大的军队

explain.en: 'He warned against joining forces with other nations because it could lead to more war. He also warned against building too big of an army.'
explain.zh: 书里说：“他警告不要与其他国家结盟，因为那可能引发更多战争。他还警告不要建立过于庞大的军队。”

q.en: Which war ended with the British surrender at Yorktown in 1781?
q.zh: 哪场战争以1781年英军在约克镇投降告终？

options.en: The American Revolution / War for Independence
French and Indian War according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 法国与印第安人战争（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: 'in 1781, Washington marched into Yorktown, Virginia, where he and the French forces surrounded the British. The British surrendered, and the war was over.'
explain.zh: 书里说：“1781年华盛顿挺进弗吉尼亚约克镇，与法军一同包围英军。英军投降，战争结束。”

q.en: Why is Washington called the 'Father of His Country'?
q.zh: 华盛顿为何被称为“国父”？

options.en: He was the first president and a model leader
options.zh: 他是首任总统，也是典范领袖

options.en: He founded a religion according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他创立了一种宗教（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: A congressman said he was 'first in war, first in peace, first in the hearts of his countrymen,' and the book calls him 'truly the Father of His Country.'
explain.zh: 一位国会议员说他“战时第一，和时第一，国人心目中第一”，书中称他“真正是国父”。

q.en: What does the book say about Washington's leadership style?
q.zh: 本书如何描述华盛顿的领导风格？

options.en: He ignored Congress according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他无视国会（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He led firmly and with great dignity, like he led the army
options.zh: 他坚定而庄重地领导，如同统率军队一般

explain.en: 'Washington's style of leadership was a good match for this task. He led the nation firmly and with great dignity—just as he had led the Continental Army.'
explain.zh: 书里说：“华盛顿的领导风格很适合这项任务。他坚定而庄重地领导国家——就像他曾统率大陆军那样。”

prompt.en: Write a journal entry from April 30, 1789, the day Washington became the first president, as if you were there.
prompt.zh: 以1789年4月30日华盛顿就任首任总统那一天为背景，写一则仿佛你亲临现场的日记。

en: Describe the crowd cheering after the oath of office.
Mention why this moment was historic (first president).
zh: 描写宣誓就职后欢呼的人群。
说明这一刻为何具有历史意义（首任总统）。
